articles.title: Why Retailers Selling Cigarettes Cannot Opt for 1% GST Composition Scheme?
articles.summary: The article explains that retailers selling cigarettes in India cannot opt for the 1% GST composition scheme, regardless of their turnover. This rule requires small cigarette retailers to comply with regular GST filing to avoid penalties. The policy is part of India's Goods and Services Tax framework, which mandates stricter compliance for certain goods like tobacco products.
articles.raw_summary: Selling cigarettes disqualifies traders from the GST composition scheme regardless of turnover. Small retailers must follow regular GST compliance to avoid penalties. articles.title: Ambiguity in NCLAT Orders Not a Complete Defence for IRP Against Regulatory Penalty: IBBI
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) ruled that confusion arising from interim orders by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) does not absolve Insolvency Resolution Professionals (IRPs) from their duty to exercise due diligence. The case highlights the need for proactive compliance with regulatory requirements in insolvency proceedings. The decision reinforces the expectation of thoroughness and accountability among IRPs under India's insolvency laws.
articles.raw_summary: The authority held that confusion arising from interim orders did not absolve the professional from taking proactive steps. The case reinforces the expectation of due diligence in insolvency proceedings. articles.summary: The Orissa High Court ruled that the Income Tax Department cannot adjust a taxpayer's refund against a disputed demand while a stay order is in place. The Court directed the department to release the refund along with interest, stating that such adjustment is illegal and arbitrary. The ruling impacts tax recovery procedures and taxpayer rights during disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that adjusting a refund against a disputed demand during the subsistence of a stay order is illegal and arbitrary, directing release of the refund with interest. articles.title: CESTAT Sets Aside ₹352 Cr Service Tax Demand as Coal Mining Held ‘Manufacture’ Not Service
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) set aside a ₹352 crore service tax demand on coal mining activities, ruling that coal extraction and processing qualify as 'manufacture' rather than a service. This decision makes the service tax demand unsustainable and clarifies the mutual exclusivity of tax levies. The ruling impacts tax policy and compliance for coal mining companies in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that coal extraction and processing constitute manufacture, not mining services, making service tax demand unsustainable and reinforcing mutual exclusivity of tax levies. articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a Magistrate must conduct an inquiry or order a probe under Section 225 of the Bhartiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 before issuing summons against an accused residing outside the court's territorial jurisdiction. The Court held that this mandatory step ensures procedural fairness and adherence to the law. The ruling clarifies the legal process for handling cases involving out-of-jurisdiction accused persons.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has held that a Magistrate must conduct an inquiry or direct an investigation under Section 225 of Bhartiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 before proceeding against an accused residing outside the territorial jurisdiction. It highlights that extended limitation periods and penalties require clear proof of intent to evade tax. The ruling impacts how tax authorities issue notices and enforce GST compliance.
articles.raw_summary: Explains that vague show cause notices lacking details of suppression violate legal principles. Highlights that extended limitation and penalties require clear proof of intent. The post GST SCNs Invalid Without Specific Allegations of Suppression appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Gratuity Deduction Can’t Be Denied for ITR–Audit Report Mismatch Without Verification
articles.summary: An Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that gratuity deductions cannot be denied solely due to mismatches between an individual's Income Tax Return (ITR) and tax audit report without proper verification. The tribunal emphasized that inadvertent reporting errors require scrutiny rather than outright rejection of deductions. This decision impacts tax compliance and corporate financial reporting practices in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal addressed disallowance arising from mismatch between ITR and tax audit report. It held that inadvertent reporting errors require verification, not outright denial of deduction. The issue is relevant to businesses and tax professionals handling export transactions.

articles.title: Polycotton Recycling Faces a Scale Test That Chemistry Alone Cannot Solve
articles.summary: The article examines the challenges of recycling polycotton blended textiles, framing it as an industrial systems problem rather than just a scientific one. Peter Mangnus of Avantium argues that solving this requires aligning feedstock, offtake, finance, and policy support. The debate underscores the need for regulatory and infrastructure interventions to address textile waste at scale. The discussion is relevant to India's textile sector and waste management policies.
articles.raw_summary: Introduction: Blended textile waste is no longer being discussed as a marginal recycling problem but as an industrial systems challenge shaped by regulation, cost and infrastructure. In that debate, Peter Mangnus , Business Director Dawn Technology at Avantium , argues that progress will depend less on scientific novelty alone than on aligning feedstock, offtake, finance and policy support.

The searches are part of an alleged money laundering probe involving Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora. The action highlights issues of financial regulation enforcement and potential policy gaps in anti-money laundering measures.

Concerns include its impact on traditional software security. Other Asian regulators are also examining these risks, signaling growing attention to AI governance in the financial sector.

articles.title: World’s first nuclear waste vault: Why Finland is burying radioactive waste 400 metres underground
articles.summary: Finland is preparing to activate the world’s first deep underground repository for highly radioactive nuclear waste, located 400 meters beneath Olkiluoto. The facility uses a multi-layered safety system to isolate waste in stable bedrock for tens of thousands of years. This project could serve as a model for other countries, including India, as they evaluate nuclear energy policies and long-term waste management strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Finland is nearing the activation of a groundbreaking underground repository for highly radioactive nuclear waste, designed for permanent storage over tens of thousands of years. This facility, built deep beneath Olkiluoto, offers a multi-layered safety approach, isolating waste in stable bedrock. It represents a potential working model for other nations as they reconsider nuclear energy.

articles.title: State of the climate: Strong El Niño puts 2026 on track for second-warmest year
articles.summary: The first three months of 2026 were the fourth warmest on record globally, with a strong El Niño event pushing 2026 toward being the second-warmest year ever. This trend highlights ongoing climate change challenges and could intensify calls for stronger environmental policies, both in India and internationally. The data underscores the urgency of addressing climate impacts, which may influence future policy decisions.

articles.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: A coalition led by France and the UK is preparing to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route for global oil supplies. The mission aims to address security concerns in the region, which could impact oil trade. The coalition has stated it will not deploy the mission until the Iran war is over. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for oil shipments, including those bound for India.
articles.raw_summary: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

articles.title: West Asia ceasefire extension fragile, risks of escalation remain: Koan Advisory Group
articles.summary: Dr. Deep Pal from Koan Advisory Group warns that the fragile ceasefire extension in West Asia could collapse, risking a sharp rise in crude oil prices. The article notes that crude prices are currently below $100 per barrel but could spike if tensions escalate or supply tightens. This situation is significant for India due to its heavy reliance on oil imports and potential economic impacts.

articles.summary: A survey in Gujarat and Rajasthan found that despite improved financial access, many households lack financial maturity. Poor understanding of compound interest and inadequate retirement planning were key issues identified.

Key issues include poor understanding of compound interest and inadequate retirement planning. Over half of respondents haven't started saving for retirement, and many rely on informal support for emergencies, highlighting a need for enhanced financial capability and resilience.

articles.title: UK inflation jumps to 3.3% in March as Iran war sends fuel prices to highest rise since Ukraine invasion
articles.summary: UK inflation rose to 3.3% in March due to a sharp increase in fuel prices, driven by geopolitical tensions from the Iran war. Motor fuel prices jumped 8.7%, and services inflation unexpectedly rose to 4.5%. This inflation outlook may impact interest rate decisions, with economists expecting prolonged pauses in rate hikes. The surge in energy costs highlights broader economic risks tied to global conflicts.

This inflation outlook may impact interest rates, with economists now expecting a prolonged pause in rate hikes.

articles.summary: A U.S. consumer watchdog group, the Consumer Federation of America, has filed a lawsuit against Meta, alleging the company profited from scam advertisements on its platforms while misleading users about its safety efforts. Internal documents cited in the lawsuit suggest Meta earns billions from such ads despite claiming to combat fraud. Meta has denied the allegations and stated it will contest the lawsuit.

articles.title: MeitY says no mandatory registration for online games without real money
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has announced new regulations exempting online games without real-money stakes from mandatory registration. Real-money games will face stricter scrutiny, with banks and payment firms required to enforce bans. Competitive e-sports will be classified separately, and user safety measures will be mandated.

articles.title: Smartphones emerge as India's export champions in FY26
articles.summary: India's electronics exports saw a significant 24% rise in the last fiscal year. Smartphones led this growth, with exports climbing nearly 22%. This surge highlights the success of the government's production linked incentive program. Key export destinations included the US, UAE, and China. This performance demonstrates India's growing role as a global manufacturing hub for electronics.

articles.title: India can grow above 7 pc even if crude oil costs USD 90-100/barrel: Assocham
articles.summary: India's economy shows resilience to high oil prices. Consumption drives growth, allowing over 7 percent annual expansion even with crude oil at USD 90-100 per barrel. Historical data supports this trend. Assocham predicts continued strong growth above 7 percent through 2026-27. This outlook contrasts with some international forecasts predicting moderation.

articles.title: Net sales growth of FDI companies moderated to 8.7% in FY25: RBI data
articles.summary: Net sales growth for select FDI companies in India moderated to 8.7% in 2024-25, down from 9.4% the previous year, according to RBI data. While services sector sales saw a slight increase, manufacturing sector growth decelerated. Operating profit growth also slowed to 10.7% due to rising expenses.

articles.title: Agriculture exports up 2.8 pc to USD 52.55 bn in 2025-26
articles.summary: India's agriculture exports are set to reach USD 52.55 billion in 2025-26, a 2.8% increase. This growth is fueled by traditional strengths and new high-value products. Marine and plantation exports are showing strong momentum. Pulses, fresh fruits, and vegetable oils are key contributors. Processed food segments are also expanding, highlighting value addition's role in boosting exports.

articles.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung's visit to New Delhi signals India's strategic push for large-scale FDI in advanced manufacturing and technology. articles.title: India seafood exports to US fall amid tariffs
articles.summary: India’s seafood exports to the US fell 19.8% in volume and 14.5% in value in FY26, hit by reciprocal tariffs, the commerce ministry said. However, overall exports hit a record $8.28 billion. Growth in China, the EU and Southeast Asia helped offset the US decline.

articles.title: Seafood exports up 11.2% in 2025-26
articles.summary: India’s seafood exports rose 11.2% to $8.28 billion in 2025–26, driven by strong demand from China, the EU and Southeast Asia. Frozen shrimp led earnings. Despite a decline in US shipments, gains in alternative markets offset losses. Export volumes hit 19.32 lakh tonnes, with key ports handling most shipments.

articles.summary: India's infrastructure investment is set for strong growth of 45-50% over the next two years. This surge is fueled by robust domestic demand and supportive government policies. Sectors like renewable energy and data centers will lead the expansion. While the West Asia conflict may cause indirect cost pressures, overall investment remains on track.

This indicates continued investor confidence in the Indian market.
